summary: Validated Google OAuth PKCE fix in routes_google.py: /google/login now persists Fernet-encrypted code_verifier keyed by sha256(state) in google_oauth_states, and /google/oauth/callback restores it via find_one_and_delete (single-use). All 6 backend tests pass. Frontend smoke on /performance also OK.

issues: Recharts warning: chart container width/height reported as -1 on initial paint (cosmetic, non-blocking)

critical_code_review_comments: routes_google.py:117 uses ISO-string comparison for created_at TTL purge; string comparison works only because all docs use the same now_iso() format. Consider a real Mongo TTL index (expireAfterSeconds=1800) on created_at for robustness.

State JWT can be identical across two rapid /google/login calls (same sub, same exp minute) → two docs with the same state_hash accumulate. Consider adding a jti/nonce claim or upserting on state_hash to avoid duplicate docs.
seed_data_creation: None (test cleans up its own google_oauth_states docs).
context_for_next_testing_agent: PKCE fix verified: authorization_url contains code_challenge=... & code_challenge_method=S256; MongoDB collection google_oauth_states stores {state_hash, code_verifier (Fernet 'enc::gAAAA...'), created_at}; callback with valid state + fake code returns HTTP 400 with 'Échec d'échange du code OAuth' but NO 'Missing code verifier'; state doc is deleted after callback (single-use). Google keys are FACTICES in preview so full OAuth handshake cannot be tested E2E.
rca of the issue: google-auth-oauthlib.Flow generates a random code_verifier at /google/login and sends its S256 hash as code_challenge. The callback previously built a fresh Flow without setting flow.code_verifier, so google's token endpoint returned 'Missing code verifier'. Fix persists the verifier (encrypted via Fernet) keyed by sha256(state) in Mongo and restores it in the callback via find_one_and_delete before flow.fetch_token().
